children.text: The Secretary may, as provided in this section, reprimand or suspend, involuntarily reassign, demote, or remove a covered individual from a senior executive position at the Department if the Secretary determines that the misconduct or performance of the covered individual warrants such action.
If the Secretary so removes such an individual, the Secretary may remove the individual from the civil service (as defined in ).section 2101 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: advance notice of the action and a file containing all evidence in support of the proposed action;

children.label: B
children.text: be represented by an attorney or other representative of the covered individual’s choice; and

children.label: C
children.text: grieve the action in accordance with an internal grievance process that the Secretary, in consultation with the Assistant Secretary for Accountability and Whistleblower Protection, shall establish for purposes of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: The aggregate period for notice, response, and decision on an action under subsection (a) may not exceed 15 business days.

children.label: B
children.text: The period for the response of a covered individual to a notice under paragraph (1)(A) of an action under subsection (a) shall be 7 business days.

children.label: C
children.text: A decision under this paragraph on an action under subsection (a) shall be issued not later than 15 business days after notice of the action is provided to the covered individual under paragraph (1)(A). The decision shall be in writing, and shall include the specific reasons therefor.
The Secretary shall ensure that the grievance process established under paragraph (1)(C) takes fewer than 21 days.
A decision under paragraph (2) that is not grieved, and a grievance decision under paragraph (3), shall be final and conclusive.
A covered individual adversely affected by a decision under paragraph (2) that is not grieved, or by a grievance decision under paragraph (3), may obtain judicial review of such decision.

children.label: A
children.text: arbitrary, capricious, an abuse of discretion, or otherwise not in accordance with a provision of law;

children.label: B
children.text: obtained without procedures required by a provision of law having been followed; or

children.label: C
children.text: unsupported by substantial evidence.

children.heading: .—Relation to Other Provisions of Law
children.label: c .—Relation to Other Provisions of Law
children.text: and the procedures under section 7543(b) of such title do not apply to an action under subsection (a).Section 3592(b)(1) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: a career appointee (as that term is defined in ); orsection 3132(a)(4) of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: any individual who occupies an administrative or executive position and who was appointed under section 7306(a), section 7401(1), or .section 7401(4) of this title
The term “misconduct” includes neglect of duty, malfeasance, or failure to accept a directed reassignment or to accompany a position in a transfer of function.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to a career appointee (as that term is defined in ), a Senior Executive Service position (as such term is defined in such section); andsection 3132(a) of title 5
